page_content:
Additional Information: Appropriate for non-science majors.

ASTR320 Theoretical Astrophysics (3 Credits)

Application of selected physics concepts in an astrophysical context. Topics would include gravity (Keplerian motion, Virial theorem, Roche limit, dynamical friction); gas dynamics (hydrostatic equilibrium, stellar models, spiral density waves), thermodynamics and statistical physics (Boltzmann distribution, Wien displacement, convective instability, degenerate gas); atomic physics (quantum principles, H atom, permitted and forbidden lines); radiation processes (line radiation, opacity). Prerequisite: ASTR121; and (PHYS270 and PHYS271; or PHYS273). Restriction: Must be in Astronomy program.
